name: El Castillo de las Guardas Loop from Aznalcóllar
name_original: El Castillo de Guardias desde Aznalcóllar – circular
description: This difficult 54.7-mile road cycling route from Aznalcóllar offers significant elevation gain and views of the Agrio Reservoir.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aznalcóllar traverse a diverse landscape in the Vía de la Plata region of Seville, Spain. The area features a combination of natural elements, including the protected Guadiamar Green Corridor, the Agrio Reservoir, and extensive pastures. Situated at the feet of mountains, the terrain offers varied elevation gains and mostly paved surfaces suitable for road cyclists. This environment provides options for both tranquil excursions and challenging rides. Best road cycling routes around Aznalcóllar The most popular road cycling route is El Castillo de las Guardas Loop from Aznalcóllar, a 54.7 miles (88.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guillena Town Hall – Las Pajanosas loop from Gerena, a moderate 33.2 miles (53.5 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride through the regional countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Super road for racing bikes – Aznalcóllar Mine loop from Gerena, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail leading through the area around the Aznalcóllar Mine, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Aznalcóllar is defined by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, the Agrio Reservoir, and rolling pastures. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Aznalcóllar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aznalcóllar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aznalcóllar?
A: There are over a dozen road cycling routes around Aznalcóllar, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate loops to more challenging rides with substantial climbs.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for road cycling in Aznalcóllar?
A: Aznalcóllar offers a good mix of road cycling routes for different abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual riders and those seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar?
A: The road cycling routes around Aznalcóllar feature mostly paved surfaces, ideal for road bikes. The landscape is diverse, traversing the protected Guadiamar Green Corridor, passing by the Agrio Reservoir, and winding through extensive pastures. You'll encounter varied elevation gains as the area is situated at the feet of mountains.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993991793/el-castillo-de-las-guardas-loop-from-aznalcollar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo de las Guardas Loop from Aznalcóllar&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, reaching up to 1,174 meters over 88 kilometers. Other challenging options include loops around the Aznalcóllar Mine and the Agrio Reservoir.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the Agrio Reservoir and the surrounding countryside. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3519784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Agrio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Guadiamar Green Corridor is a prominent ecological feature you'll encounter, known for its rich flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, the area boasts several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3519784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Agrio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning perspectives of the water and landscape. Additionally, the Mirador de la Cárcava (Balcón del Guadiamar) provides panoramic views of the Guadiamar river, olive groves, and surrounding municipalities.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar?
A: While some routes feature significant climbs, there are moderate options that families with older children or those seeking a less strenuous ride might enjoy. The Guadiamar Green Corridor, with its protected and scenic environment, often provides smoother sections suitable for a more relaxed pace. For specific easy routes, check the guide for options with lower elevation gains.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33980575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena Town Hall – Las Pajanosas loop from Gerena&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38286664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Super road for racing bikes – Aznalcóllar Mine loop from Gerena&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular journeys through the regional countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aznalcóllar?
A: The Vía de la Plata region, where Aznalcóllar is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring and autumn typically provide mild weather, making them ideal for enjoying the routes. Winter cycling is also possible, though cooler temperatures should be expected.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Aznalcóllar?
A: The komoot community rates the road cycling routes in Aznalcóllar highly, with an average score of 4.3 stars from numerous reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which includes the scenic Guadiamar Green Corridor and views of the Agrio Reservoir, as well as the variety of routes catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Aznalcóllar offers some historical points that can be incorporated into your cycling excursions. You might find remains of a Roman aqueduct, the unique Mudejar chapel known as the Zawiya, or the ruins of a medieval castle, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: Where can I park my car when going road cycling in Aznalcóllar?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Aznalcóllar, as a town, typically offers public parking options. Many routes start from or pass through the town, making it a convenient base. For routes that begin outside the town, local information or komoot's route details often provide insights into suitable starting points with parking.
tips: The municipality known above all for its pyrite mines (exploited since ancient times by the Romans) temporarily closed after one of the biggest environmental disasters in Europe - when in 1998 the Aznalcóllar mining dam burst and caused a polluting spill in this area.
Views of the Agrio river reservoir - its name comes from the taste of its waters, caused by the mining that has occurred in its basin for thousands of years.
Totally lonely road, good road surface

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Castillo de Guardias from Aznalcóllar circular route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is quite demanding, covering nearly 88 kilometers with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Most cyclists can expect to complete it in approximately 4 hours and 11 minutes of active riding time, though this can vary based on fitness and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads suitable for racebikes, connecting the former mining town of Aznalcóllar with El Castillo de las Guardas. You'll experience a mix of natural landscapes, including large pastures, views of the Agrio Reservoir, and traditional dehesas (oak pastures). While the route is designed for road cycling, the area generally features diverse rural environments.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass by several notable spots. Highlights include the historic Aznalcóllar Mine, offering a glimpse into the region's past. You'll also enjoy a View of the Agrio Reservoir, and ride along a Scenic Road Near Aznalcóllar. The route also takes you through areas known for their natural beauty, such as the Guadiamar Green Corridor and the rural environment of El Castillo de las Guardas.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of nearly 88 kilometers and significant elevation gain of over 1,100 meters, this route is officially graded as difficult. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride with considerable climbs and descents, rather than beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Aznalcóllar?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Aznalcóllar serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find public parking within the town to begin your ride. It's always a good idea to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads and established paths. You are free to enjoy the ride without any special permissions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Aznalcóllar and El Castillo de las Guardas is best enjoyed during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Is the El Castillo de Guardias from Aznalcóllar circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's important to consider the safety of your pet on a long, difficult road cycling route with traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured and that you comply with local leash laws and waste disposal regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will briefly encounter parts of the "Antigua linea ferrea Peña de Hierro - (Ronquillo) - San Juan de Aznalfarache" and the "Camino a la Fuente La Sebastiana" trails. The area around El Castillo de las Guardas also boasts 70 kilometers of signposted paths suitable for various types of cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Both Aznalcóllar and El Castillo de las Guardas are towns with local amenities. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and shops in these towns where you can refuel and rest. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What unique natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: Beyond the reservoir views, the route traverses areas rich in natural beauty. You'll experience the traditional dehesas, which are characteristic oak pastures of the region, and pass through parts of the Guadiamar Green Corridor. Near El Castillo de las Guardas, there's also a significant animal reserve, one of Europe's largest, where animals live in semi-freedom, located just 5 kilometers from the town center.
